title: JJThunder To The Max
description: Terra 1-1 styled world generation datapack for latest versions
body:

# Discord:

I myself am a lone developer for this data pack, however, Raytraceforge recently opened up a discord channel specifically for all JJThunder data packs.

### Link:
https://discord.gg/raytrace-forge-1122227053752746104


# Compatibility:

Right now, no direct compatibility is available with other world generation mods and data packs:

- Data packs like Terralith and William Wythers can run with my data pack, however, all biomes layout and surfaces are messed up, so not compatible.
  
- Biomes mods like biomes o plenty and Oh the biomes we've gone can also run with my data pack, but snow will cover all new biome surfaces, and biome layout is inconsistent with world generation, so also not compatible.


# Roadmap:

- Due to major success of v0.5.0, I would greatly slow down world generation updates, and will focus more on supporting versions from 1.19.x and above.

- For other developers hosting world generation mods and data packs, I would like to show interest in compatibility between JJThunder data packs and your work, so please don't hesitate to contact me on discord.

# Summary:

JJThunder To the Max introduces a whole new world generation experience based on JJThunder King of the Hills. It adds mountains that are 1900 blocks tall as well as flat plains, deep oceans, and smooth transitions.

This data pack also introduces new noise caves that replaces old ones. They start out small and claustrophobic, while growing with depth to reach a whole new world generation (The Underlands). Only the Underlands are endless while the other types of caves are not.

# World Generation:

### **Slopes:**

* Oceans
* Flat Plains
* Hills
* Extreme Hills
* Mountains
* Extreme Mountains


### **Height:**

From y=-64 to y=2032


### **Caves:**

* Small Caves
* Medium Caves
* Large Caves
* The Underlands

# Must read:

* Allocate at least 8 GB of RAM.

* Compatible with performance mods, if you set them as priority in data pack folder. Order of mods/datapacks in the data pack folder matters, so put my datapack above default minecraft, but under all other performance mods.

* With proper performance mods installed and enough RAM allocated, FPS and TPS will not be a problem. Only Chunk rendering will be slow, due to lack of cubic chunks loading.

* Does not work with existing worlds. It will severely damage existing ones, so back them up before using my datapack. 

* No known compatibilities with other world generation mods/datapacks found. Would like to work on compatibilities when other developers consent.
